name: Georgian Khachapuri
steps: Make a soft dough from the flour and water, and roll into a round.
Fill with the crumbled cheese, fold the edges up into a boat shape, and crack the egg into the center.
Bake at 425F for 15-18 minutes until golden, with the egg just set.
steps_detailed: Make a soft, elastic dough from the flour, baking powder, a pinch of salt, and water. Rest 20 minutes.
Roll into an oval, fill generously with the crumbled cheese, and fold the edges up and twist the ends to form a boat shape, leaving the center open.
Bake at 425F (220C) for 12-15 minutes, then crack the egg into the center and top with the butter, baking 3-5 more minutes until the egg is just set.
Khachapuri, cheese-filled bread, is Georgia's most iconic dish, with countless regional variations - this is the Adjarian, boat-shaped style.
description: Khachapuri, cheese-filled bread, is Georgia's most iconic dish, with countless regional variations - this is the Adjarian, boat-shaped style.

name: Georgian Shoti with Cheese
steps: Warm the bread.
Serve with the cheese for a simple breakfast.
steps_detailed: Warm the bread (standing in for shoti, Georgia's traditional boat-shaped bread) until soft.
Serve with the cheese and cilantro - a simple, everyday Georgian breakfast pairing.
description: Shoti, a boat-shaped bread traditionally baked in a tone (clay oven), paired here with cheese.

name: Georgian Walnut and Honey Toast
steps: Toast the bread.
Top with the walnuts and drizzle with honey.
steps_detailed: Toast the bread until golden.
Spread with the cream, top with the walnuts, and drizzle generously with honey - a simple, sweet Georgian breakfast, walnuts being central to Georgian cuisine.
description: Walnuts, central to Georgian cooking, paired simply with honey here.

name: Georgian Khinkali
steps: Make a simple dough from the flour and water, and roll into small rounds.
Mix the beef with the onion and pepper, and place a spoonful in the center of each round.
Gather the edges up and twist to seal into a small pouch, then boil for 10-12 minutes.
steps_detailed: Make a soft, firm dough from the flour, a pinch of salt, and water, and roll out, cutting into small rounds.
Mix the beef with the onion, pepper, cumin, and cilantro, and a splash of water to keep it juicy.
Place a spoonful of filling in the center of each round, gather the edges up and pleat, twisting to seal into a small pouch with a distinctive topknot.
Boil in batches 10-14 minutes until they float and are cooked through - khinkali, Georgia's beloved soup dumplings, are traditionally eaten by hand, holding the topknot.
description: Khinkali, Georgia's beloved soup dumplings, traditionally eaten by hand, holding the topknot.

name: Georgian Lobio
steps: Simmer the beans with the onion and garlic in their liquid plus a splash of water for 15-18 minutes.
Stir in the cilantro and serve with bread.
steps_detailed: Saute the onion and garlic in the oil with the coriander until softened, 5-6 minutes.
Add the beans with their liquid and a splash of water, and simmer 18-22 minutes until thickened.
Stir in the cilantro and serve with warm bread - lobio, a beloved bean stew, is a Georgian staple, especially popular during fasting periods.
description: Lobio, a bean stew, one of Georgia's most common everyday dishes.

name: Georgian Pkhali

ingredients.note: as a spinach/beet-green stand-in, finely chopped

steps: Blanch the cabbage until tender, then squeeze out excess moisture.
Blend with the ground walnuts, garlic, and vinegar into a thick paste.
Shape into small mounds and serve chilled.

ingredients_full.note: as a spinach/beet-green stand-in, finely chopped

steps_detailed: Blanch the cabbage (standing in for spinach or beet greens, traditionally used) until tender, then drain thoroughly and squeeze out as much moisture as possible.
Blend with the ground walnuts, garlic, and vinegar into a thick, cohesive paste.
description: Shape into small mounds, garnish with pistachios, and serve chilled - pkhali, walnut-bound vegetable pâté, is a beloved Georgian mezze dish.

name: Georgian Ojakhuri
steps: Fry the pork with the onion in the oil until browned.
Add the potato and continue frying until everything is tender and golden, about 20-25 minutes.
steps_detailed: Fry the pork with the onion and garlic in the oil over medium-high heat, 8-10 minutes, until browned.
Add the potato and continue frying, stirring occasionally, 22-28 minutes until everything is tender and deeply golden.
Top with cilantro - ojakhuri, meaning 'family-style,' is a hearty, everyday Georgian skillet dish.
description: Ojakhuri, meaning 'family style,' a hearty fried meat and potato dish.

name: Georgian Chakhokhbili
steps: Brown the chicken with the onion.
Add the tomatoes and tarragon, and simmer 25-30 minutes until tender.
Serve with bread.
steps_detailed: Brown the chicken with the onion and garlic over medium-high heat, 8-10 minutes.
Add the tomatoes and tarragon, and simmer, covered, 28-35 minutes until the chicken is tender and the sauce has thickened.
description: Top with cilantro and serve with bread - chakhokhbili, a tomato and herb chicken stew, is a beloved Georgian home-cooked dish.

name: Georgian Satsivi
steps: Simmer the chicken until tender, then shred it, reserving the broth.
Blend the ground walnuts with the reserved broth, onion, and garlic into a thick sauce.
Toss the shredded chicken with the sauce and chill before serving.
steps_detailed: Simmer the chicken with the onion in water until fully tender, 25-30 minutes, then shred it finely, reserving the broth.
Blend the ground walnuts with 1/2 cup of the reserved broth, garlic, coriander, and vinegar into a thick, smooth sauce.

Toss the shredded chicken with the walnut sauce and chill at least 30 minutes before serving - satsivi, chicken in a rich walnut sauce, is a treasured Georgian dish, especially for New Year celebrations.
description: Satsivi, chicken in a rich walnut sauce, a treasured Georgian dish especially for New Year celebrations.

name: Georgian Kharcho

ingredients.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps: Simmer the beef until nearly tender, about 1 hour.
Add the rice, tomatoes, and ground walnuts, and simmer 20-25 more minutes until the rice is tender.
Serve hot.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps_detailed: Simmer the beef in 2.5 cups water for 1-1.25 hours until nearly tender.
Add the rice, tomatoes, ground walnuts, and garlic, and simmer 22-28 more minutes until the rice is tender and the soup has thickened.
description: Top with cilantro and serve hot - kharcho, a spicy, walnut-thickened beef and rice soup, is a beloved Georgian dish.

name: Georgian Chikhirtma
steps: Simmer the chicken with the onion until tender, about 25-30 minutes, then shred it.
Whisk the egg with the lime juice, then slowly whisk in a ladle of the hot broth to temper it.
Stir the tempered egg back into the pot and warm gently without boiling.
steps_detailed: Simmer the chicken with the onion in the butter and 2.5 cups water for 28-35 minutes until tender, then shred it and return to the pot.
Whisk the egg with the lime juice, then slowly whisk in a ladle of the hot broth to temper it and prevent curdling.
Stir the tempered egg mixture back into the pot and warm gently over low heat, without letting it boil, until slightly thickened.
Top with cilantro - chikhirtma, an egg-lemon chicken soup, is a soothing, traditional Georgian dish.
description: Chikhirtma, a chicken soup thickened and enriched with egg, similar in spirit to Greek avgolemono.

name: Georgian Badrijani Nigvzit
steps: Fry the eggplant slices until golden.
Blend the ground walnuts with the garlic and a splash of water into a thick paste.
Spread the walnut paste onto the eggplant slices and roll up.
steps_detailed: Fry the eggplant slices in the oil over medium heat, 3-5 minutes per side, until golden and tender.
Blend the ground walnuts with the garlic, vinegar, and a splash of water into a thick, smooth paste.
Spread the walnut paste generously onto each eggplant slice and roll up into small logs, garnishing with pistachios.
description: Badrijani nigvzit, walnut-stuffed eggplant rolls, are a beloved Georgian appetizer.

name: Georgian Mtsvadi
steps: Mix the beef with the onion and pepper, and shape into small logs.
Grill or pan-fry until well charred and cooked through.
steps_detailed: Mix the beef thoroughly with the onion, pepper, garlic, and cilantro, and shape into small logs around skewers if you have them.
Grill or pan-fry, 4-6 minutes per side, until well charred and cooked through.
description: Mtsvadi, grilled skewered meat, is a beloved Georgian dish, central to festive gatherings and countryside cookouts.

name: Georgian Churchkhela
steps: Thread the walnut halves onto a skewer.
Simmer the grape juice with the flour until thick, then dip the skewer repeatedly, letting it dry between dips.
Let dry fully before eating.
steps_detailed: Thread the walnut halves onto a skewer, forming a short string.
Whisk the flour into the grape juice and sugar, and simmer, stirring constantly, 8-10 minutes until thick like a pudding.
Dip the walnut skewer into the thickened juice repeatedly, letting each layer dry slightly before the next, building up a thick candy coating.
description: Let dry fully, at least several hours, before eating - churchkhela, walnuts in a grape-juice candy coating, is a beloved traditional Georgian sweet, sometimes called 'Georgian Snickers.'

name: Georgian Kupati
steps: Mix the beef with the garlic and coriander, and shape into small patties.
Pan-fry in the oil until well browned and cooked through.
steps_detailed: Mix the beef thoroughly with the garlic, coriander, black pepper, and tarragon, and shape into small patties or short logs.
Pan-fry in the oil over medium heat, 5-7 minutes per side, until well browned and cooked through.
description: Kupati, spiced Georgian sausage, is a beloved dish, especially popular grilled over an open fire.

name: Georgian Gozinaki
steps: Warm the honey until it thins slightly.
Stir in the walnuts, coating well.
Press into a small dish and chill before cutting into pieces.
steps_detailed: Warm the honey with the cinnamon over low heat, 2-3 minutes, until it thins slightly.
Stir in the walnuts, coating thoroughly.
Press firmly into a small lined dish and chill at least 1 hour until firm.
Cut into diamond shapes - gozinaki, a simple honey-walnut candy, is a traditional Georgian New Year's treat.
description: Gozinaki, walnuts set in honey, traditionally made for New Year.

name: Georgian Honey Cake
steps: Cream the butter and honey, then beat in the egg.
Fold in the flour and pour into a small buttered dish.
Bake at 350F for 20-25 minutes until golden and springy.
steps_detailed: Cream the butter and honey together until light, then beat in the egg and cinnamon.
Fold in the flour gently until just combined, and pour into a small buttered baking dish.
Bake at 350F (175C) for 22-28 minutes until golden and springy to the touch.
A simple, honey-forward Georgian cake, honey playing a central role in Georgian sweets.
description: A simple honey cake.

name: Georgian Pelamushi
steps: Whisk the flour into the grape juice.
Simmer, stirring constantly, 8-10 minutes until thick like a pudding.
Pour into small cups, top with walnuts, and chill until set.
steps_detailed: Whisk the flour into the grape juice until smooth, no lumps remaining.
Simmer over medium-low heat, stirring constantly, 10-12 minutes until thick and pudding-like.
Pour into small cups, top with the chopped walnuts and a dusting of cinnamon, and chill at least 1 hour until set.
description: Pelamushi, a simple grape juice pudding, shares its base with churchkhela and is a beloved traditional Georgian dessert, especially around grape harvest season.

name: Georgian Tkemali

ingredients.ingredient: Cranberries, dried, sweetened (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps: Simmer the cranberries with a splash of water until soft, then blend with the garlic, cilantro, and vinegar.
Serve alongside grilled meats.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Cranberries, dried, sweetened (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps_detailed: Simmer the cranberries with a splash of water for 5-6 minutes until soft, then blend with the garlic, cilantro, vinegar, and coriander into a smooth, tangy sauce.
This nods to tkemali, a beloved Georgian sour plum sauce, essential alongside mtsvadi and other grilled meats.
description: Tkemali, a tart plum sauce used across Georgian cooking much the way ketchup is used elsewhere.

name: Georgian Ajika
steps: Blend the jalapeno, garlic, cilantro, and coriander into a coarse, fiery paste.
Season with salt and serve alongside grilled meats and khinkali.
steps_detailed: Blend the jalapeno, garlic, cilantro, coriander, and tarragon into a coarse, vibrant, fiery paste.
Season with salt - ajika, a bold herb and pepper paste, is a foundational Georgian condiment.
description: Ajika, a spicy pepper paste, a defining Georgian condiment.
